---
title: "cow_HW4_r"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-01"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Load libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)
library(scales)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(pbkrtest)
library(emmeans)
library(rstatix)
library(forcats)
library(scales) 
```


# Read in metadata
```{r}
metadata <- read_tsv("ADD YOUR FILE PATH HERE")
```
################################################################################
# Alpha Diversity
################################################################################

# Read in alpha diversity data 
```{r}
# Alpha diversity metrics
shannon <- read_tsv("ADD YOUR FILE PATH HERE")
```


# Tidy alpha diversity metrics and add to metadata
```{r}
# Shannon 
shannon <- shannon %>%
  rename(sample_name = 1)  #rename 1st column to to match metadata


# Add to metadata
metadata <- metadata %>%
  left_join(shannon, by = "sample_name") 
  
```

# Tidy the metadata 
```{r}
metadata <- metadata %>% 
  drop_na(shannon_entropy)
```

# Linear Mixed Effects Models

Shannon
```{r}
# Build model
model_bodysite_shannon <- lmer(shannon_entropy ~ body_site + (1|cow_id), data = metadata) # I name all my models with the variable I am testing and the metric I am testing. 

# Residual diagnostic plot, check assumptions
plot(model_bodysite_shannon)
# look at estimated variance components
summary(model_bodysite_shannon)
# residual diagnostic plot, check assumptions
anova(model_bodysite_shannon)
# pairwise comparisons, there are a lot of time points for this data set so this line doesn't work yet, I'll show you how to use it a little later
emmeans(model_bodysite_shannon, pairwise ~ body_site)
```

# Build shannon figure with p-values 
```{r}

# Estimated marginal means pairwise comparisons
shannon_emmeans <- emmeans(model_bodysite_shannon, ~ body_site)

pvals_shannon <- pairs(shannon_emmeans) |> 
  as.data.frame()

# Keep only significant comparisons
pvals_shannon_significant <- pvals_shannon %>%
  separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.05) %>%
  mutate(
    p = if_else(
      p.value < 0.0001,
      "< 0.0001",
      sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
    )
  )

pvals_shannon_significant

# Fix overlapping significance bars for plotting
pvals_shannon_significant <- pvals_shannon_significant %>%
  add_y_position(
    data = metadata,
    formula = shannon_entropy ~ body_site,
    fun = "max",
    step.increase = 0.12
  )

pvals_shannon_significant

# Plot Shannon by body site with only significant emmeans comparisons
bodysite_shannon_plot <- ggplot(
  metadata,
  aes(x = body_site, y = shannon_entropy, fill = body_site)
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pvals_shannon_significant,
    xmin = "group1",
    xmax = "group2",
    label = "p",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 3,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Body Site",
    y = "Shannon Entropy",
    title = "Shannon Entropy Across Body Sites"
  )

bodysite_shannon_plot
```

# Save the shannon body site figure
```{r}
ggsave(bodysite_shannon_plot, filename = "../05_figures/shannon_bodysite.jpeg", device = "jpeg", dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6)
```


################################################################################
# Beta Diversity
################################################################################

# Read in beta diversity data
```{r}
# Read in beta div metrics 
uw_unifrac <- read_tsv("ADD YOUR FILE PATH HERE")
```

# Tidy beta diversity data and make a unweighted unifrac metadata
```{r}
uw_var <- uw_unifrac %>%
  slice(3) %>%
  separate(names(.)[2], into = paste0("PC", 1:10), sep = "\t", extra = "drop") %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("PC"), ~ as.numeric(.x) * 100)) %>%
  select(PC1, PC2, PC3)

# Clean ordinance
uw_unifrac <- uw_unifrac %>%
  slice(6:n()) %>%
  filter(!.[[1]] %in% c("Biplot", "Site constraints")) %>%
  separate(names(.)[2], into = paste0("PC", 1:10), sep = "\t", extra = "drop") %>%
  rename(sample_name = 1)

metadata_uwunifrac <- uw_unifrac %>%
  select(sample_name, PC1, PC2, PC3) %>%
  left_join(metadata) %>% 
  mutate(across(c(PC1, PC2, PC3), as.numeric))
```

# Unweighted Unifrac

PC1
```{r}
# Build model
model_bodysite_uwunifrac1 <- lmer(PC1 ~ body_site + (1|cow_id), data = metadata_uwunifrac)  
# I always add which PC I am testing into the model name

# Residual diagnostic plot, check assumptions
plot(model_bodysite_uwunifrac1)
# look at estimated variance components
summary(model_bodysite_uwunifrac1)
# residual diagnostic plot, check assumptions
anova(model_bodysite_uwunifrac1)
# pairwise comparisons
emmeans(model_bodysite_uwunifrac1, pairwise ~ body_site)
```
PC2
```{r}
# Build model
model_bodysite_uwunifrac2 <- lmer(PC2 ~ body_site + (1|cow_id), data = metadata_uwunifrac)  
# I always add which PC I am testing into the model name

# Residual diagnostic plot, check assumptions
plot(model_bodysite_uwunifrac2)
# look at estimated variance components
summary(model_bodysite_uwunifrac2)
# residual diagnostic plot, check assumptions
anova(model_bodysite_uwunifrac2)
# pairwise comparisons
emmeans(model_bodysite_uwunifrac2, pairwise ~ body_site)
```
PC3
```{r}
# Build model
model_bodysite_uwunifrac3 <- lmer(PC3 ~ body_site + (1|cow_id), data = metadata_uwunifrac)  
# I always add which PC I am testing into the model name

# Residual diagnostic plot, check assumptions
plot(model_bodysite_uwunifrac3)
# look at estimated variance components
summary(model_bodysite_uwunifrac3)
# residual diagnostic plot, check assumptions
anova(model_bodysite_uwunifrac3)
# pairwise comparisons
emmeans(model_bodysite_uwunifrac3, pairwise ~ body_site)
```


# Unweighted Unifrac Pcoa
- For the Unweighted UniFrac PCoA, the p-values were taken from the ANOVA for each principal coordinate because there were too many body-site comparisons to interpret pairwise. Overall, significant ANOVA results indicate that microbial community composition differed across body sites along those axes. 
```{r}
# Pull ANOVA p-values for body_site from each PC model
p_pc1 <- anova(model_bodysite_uwunifrac1)["body_site", "Pr(>F)"]
p_pc2 <- anova(model_bodysite_uwunifrac2)["body_site", "Pr(>F)"]
p_pc3 <- anova(model_bodysite_uwunifrac3)["body_site", "Pr(>F)"]

# Format p-values
format_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(p < 0.0001, "< 0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", p))
}

p_label <- paste0(
  "PC1: p = ", format_p(p_pc1), "\n",
  "PC2: p = ", format_p(p_pc2), "\n",
  "PC3: p = ", format_p(p_pc3)
)

# Bottom-right placement
x_max <- max(metadata_uwunifrac$PC1, na.rm = TRUE)
y_min <- min(metadata_uwunifrac$PC2, na.rm = TRUE)

# Plot by body site
bodysite_uwunifrac_pcoa <- ggplot(
  metadata_uwunifrac,
  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = body_site)
) + 
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_bw() + 
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95) +
  xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(uw_var[1], 1), "%)")) + 
  ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(uw_var[2], 1), "%)")) +
  labs(
    title = "Unweighted Unifrac PCoA",
    color = "Body Site"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = x_max,
    y = y_min,
    label = p_label,
    hjust = 0.3,
    vjust = 0,
    size = 3,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")

bodysite_uwunifrac_pcoa

```

# Save the unweighted unifrac pcoa
```{r}
ggsave(bodysite_uwunifrac_pcoa, filename = "../05_figures/unweighted_unifrac_bodysite_pcoa.jpeg", device = "jpeg", dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6)
```

################################################################################
# Taxa bar plots
################################################################################

# Load in tabulated_results.tsv
```{r}
tabulated_results <- read_tsv("ADD YOUR FILE PATH HERE")
```


# Format, convert taxonomy to relative abundance, and group at taxonomic levels
```{r}
# format data for making a taxa plot
# get rid of #q2:types row
tabulated_results <- tabulated_results[2:nrow(tabulated_results),]

# remove non-numeric columns
# we will filter out Taxon for now so we can calculate relative abundances, but will add it back later
tabulated_results_filt <- tabulated_results %>% 
  select(-all_of(c("Sequence","Confidence","Taxon")))
# we keep id and make it as the row name
tabulated_results_filt <- column_to_rownames(tabulated_results_filt, var = "id")

# calculate relative abundances
tabulated_results_filt[] <- lapply(tabulated_results_filt, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))
rel_abundance <- apply(tabulated_results_filt, 2, function(x) x / sum(x))

# check that each column adds up to 1
colSums(rel_abundance)

# re-add taxonomy to relative abundance table
rel_abundance <- data.frame(rel_abundance, check.names = FALSE)
rel_abundance$id <- rownames(rel_abundance)

tabulated_results_merge <- tabulated_results %>% # create a tabulated results for merging
  select(all_of(c("id","Taxon")))

rel_abundance_taxa <- merge(rel_abundance, tabulated_results_merge, by = "id", all.x = TRUE)

# we now have a table with the per-sample relative abundances of each ASV (along with their taxonomic assignments)

# Taxon column data is messy - let's split the data up into taxonomic ranks
rel_abundance_taxa_split <- rel_abundance_taxa %>% 
  separate(Taxon, 
           into = c("Domain", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"),
           sep = ";\\s*",  # splits on semicolon and optional space
           fill = "right", # fills missing columns with NA if some rows are shorter
           remove = TRUE) # remove original "Taxon" column too

# some ranks have a value even though they are unidentified (e.g., g__, s__)
# let's change those to NAs
rel_abundance_taxa_split[rel_abundance_taxa_split == "d__"] <- NA
rel_abundance_taxa_split[rel_abundance_taxa_split == "p__"] <- NA
rel_abundance_taxa_split[rel_abundance_taxa_split == "c__"] <- NA
rel_abundance_taxa_split[rel_abundance_taxa_split == "o__"] <- NA
rel_abundance_taxa_split[rel_abundance_taxa_split == "f__"] <- NA
rel_abundance_taxa_split[rel_abundance_taxa_split == "g__"] <- NA
rel_abundance_taxa_split[rel_abundance_taxa_split == "s__"] <- NA

# create a column with the lowest identified taxonomic level for all ASVs
rel_abundance_taxa_lowest <- rel_abundance_taxa_split %>% 
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(LowestTaxLevel = {
    tax_levels <- c(Domain, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species)
    last_non_na <- tail(na.omit(tax_levels), 1)
    ifelse(length(last_non_na) > 0, last_non_na, NA)
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

# group by the lowest taxonomic level
rel_abundance_taxa_lowest <- rel_abundance_taxa_lowest %>%
  mutate(LowestTaxLevel = ifelse(is.na(LowestTaxLevel), "Unassigned", LowestTaxLevel)) %>% # convert NAs to "Unassigned"
  group_by(LowestTaxLevel) %>%
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), sum, na.rm = TRUE))

# save the file
saveRDS(rel_abundance_taxa_lowest, "taxonomy/rel_abundance_taxa_lowest.RDS")

# group at level 5/Family level
rel_abundance_taxa_family <- rel_abundance_taxa_split %>%
  mutate(Family = ifelse(is.na(Family), "Unassigned", Family)) %>% # convert NAs to "Unassigned"
  group_by(Family) %>%
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), sum, na.rm = TRUE))

# save the file
saveRDS(rel_abundance_taxa_family, "taxonomy/rel_abundance_taxa_family.RDS")

# this allows you to have either the lowest-identified level or collapsed taxonomic tables 
# however, these are massive and have >400 samples for this dataset
# because this isn't very conducive to nice taxa bar plots, in the next chunk, we are going to filter to one facility only and make a simple taxa bar plot
```

# Taxa bar plot for fecal and body site
```{r}
# graph at the family level for fecal samples
# convert to long format
rel_abundance_taxa_family_long <- rel_abundance_taxa_family %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Family, names_to = "sample_name", values_to = "relative_abundance")

# assign rare taxa (less than .02 rel abund to other)
rel_abundance_taxa_family_long <- rel_abundance_taxa_family_long %>%
  mutate(rare_taxa = if_else(relative_abundance < 0.02, "Other", Family))

# associate metadata with taxa
metadata <- read_tsv("../03_metadata/cow_metadata.txt")
metadata_filt <- metadata[, which((names(metadata) %in% c("sample_name","body_site"))==TRUE)]

rel_abundance_taxa_family_long <- rel_abundance_taxa_family_long %>% 
  left_join(metadata_filt, by = "sample_name")

# filter out control samples
rel_abundance_taxa_family_long <- rel_abundance_taxa_family_long[rel_abundance_taxa_family_long$body_site != "control",]

# filter to just one body site
rel_abundance_taxa_family_long_fecal <- rel_abundance_taxa_family_long[rel_abundance_taxa_family_long$body_site == "fecal",]

base_colors <- c(
  "#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3", "#E7298A", "#66A61E",
  "#C06C84", "#A6761D", "#666666", "#7f3b6c", "#355c7d",
  "#b15928", "#9e003f", "#ff7f00", "#8dd3c7", "#bebada",
  "#fb8072", "#80b1d3", "#fdb462", "#b3de69", "#fccde5",
  "#d9d9d9", "#bc80bd", "#ccebc5", "#ffed6f", "#a6cee3"
)

# Get unique taxa categories from the full dataset if you want to use colors for both plots
categories <- unique(rel_abundance_taxa_family_long$rare_taxa)

# special colors for Other and Unassigned
special_colors <- c("Other" = "gray", "Unassigned" = "black")
remaining <- setdiff(categories, names(special_colors))

# Recycle colors properly
assigned_colors <- rep(base_colors, length.out = length(remaining))
names(assigned_colors) <- remaining

# Combine special and base colors
colors <- c(special_colors, assigned_colors)

#taxa bar plot, plotted at the Family taxonomic level 
taxa_family_fecal <- ggplot(
  rel_abundance_taxa_family_long_fecal,
  aes(x = sample_name, y = relative_abundance, fill = rare_taxa)
) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Sample",
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Family")

taxa_family_fecal
ggsave(taxa_family_fecal, filename = "../05_figures/taxa_family_fecal.jpeg", device = "jpeg", dpi = 300, width = 12, height = 8)

# Taxabar plot for body site
# graph taxa by sample type at the family level 
rel_abundance_taxa_family_long_fecal_ordered <- rel_abundance_taxa_family_long_fecal %>%
  mutate(body_site = fct_relevel(body_site, "fecal","nasal", "oral", "skin", "udder")) %>% 
  arrange(body_site)

taxa_family_bodysite <- ggplot(
  rel_abundance_taxa_family_long,
  aes(x = sample_name, y = relative_abundance, fill = rare_taxa)) +
  facet_wrap(~body_site, scales = "free_x") +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "Sample", y = "Relative Abundance", fill = "Family")

taxa_family_bodysite

ggsave(taxa_family_bodysite, filename = "../05_figures/taxa_family_bodysite.jpeg", device = "jpeg", dpi = 300, width = 12, height = 8)
```
